Evidence Text: Liquid chromatography at critical condition (LCCC) was conducted with a single C18 bonded silica column (Nucleosil bare silica, 50 Å pore, 250 x 4.6 mm, 5 mm bead size), following the procedures as reported [29,48e50]. Notes: The text mentions 'C18 bonded silica' but immediately clarifies the column is 'Nucleosil bare silica'. The mobile phase (THF/hexane) and bare silica stationary phase indicate Normal Phase LCCC. The condition is used to separate cyclic PS from linear PS contaminants.

Physical Address: Department of Chemistry and Polymer Research Institute, POSTECH, Pohang, 790-784 KOREA
Critical Condition Basis: Chromatographic critical point where enthalpic and entropic terms compensate, resulting in elution independent of molecular weight
Evidence Text: At the composition of 57/43 (v/v) CH2Cl2 /CH3CN, all the PS standards are eluted as a single peak. This is the chromatographic critical point or the adsorption-size exclusion transition point where the enthalpic and entropic terms in Eq. (2) compensate each other. This compensation results in the loss of separation selectivity according to the polymer molecular weight, and all the PS standards are eluted near the injection solvent peak position regardless of their molecular weights.
Notes: Critical condition identified for Polystyrene at 30.5 C with 57/43 CH2Cl2/CH3CN. Also mentioned as the critical condition observed at 30.5 C in Fig 3.

Physical Address: Department of Chemistry and Polymer Research Institute, POSTECH, Pohang, 790-784 KOREA
Critical Condition Basis: Chromatographic critical point where enthalpic and entropic terms compensate, resulting in elution independent of molecular weight
Evidence Text: At the composition of 57/43 (v/v) CH2Cl2 /CH3CN, all the PS standards are eluted as a single peak. This is the chromatographic critical point or the adsorption-size exclusion transition point where the enthalpic and entropic terms in Eq. (2) compensate each other. This compensation results in the loss of separation selectivity according to the polymer molecular weight, and all the PS standards are eluted near the injection solvent peak position regardless of their molecular weights.
Notes: Critical condition identified for Polystyrene at 30.5 C with 57/43 CH2Cl2/CH3CN. Also mentioned as the critical condition observed at 30.5 C in Fig 3.
